title: A new trick for old science, and  biotech VCs’ scrambled playbook
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD": Why is old exon science getting new traction? What’s unsettling biotech VCs? And who will be the next CEO of PhRMA?

title: Invivyd reveals plans to test an antibody drug against measles
summary: The drug, which Invivyd sees as a potential treatment and “targeted alternative” for the vaccine-hesitant, comes as measles cases in the U.S. are reaching levels not seen in decades.

title: Chasing a tough-to-treat lung disease, Avalyn plans an IPO

title: STAT+: A new health care blueprint from a key Democratic think tank
summary: Democrats are focused on health care affordability as costs keep climbing.

title: Lilly launches GLP-1 pill, kicking off showdown with Novo Nordisk
summary: After winning the battle for injectable obesity medicines, Lilly hopes Foundayo can help it catch up in the oral market, where Novo has a three-month lead.

title: STAT+: 5 years after lupus breakthrough, CAR-T is still surprising autoimmunity researchers
summary: CAR-T therapy is toting up more remissions in autoimmune disease, and generating a flood of experimentation and investment.

summary: In this edition of Health Tech: FDA rejects a proposal to deregulate certain types of AI devices, pharma invests $50 million in DTx, and more.

summary: In December, Trump said he would summon health insurance execs to the White House. It doesn't seem a meeting ever happened.

summary: MAHA ELEVATE could open a federal funding pipeline to interventions that can't survive contact with a plausibility filter.

summary: For decades, venture capitalists have relied on a tried-and-true recipe to make money in biotech. Now that approach is being disrupted.

summary: “We can’t afford another sweeping restructuring of the food supply based on imprecise interventions and uncertain science,” writes David L. Ludwig.

summary: “War may command attention, but welfare is built in laboratories,” write Charentan Chatterjee and Guy Vernet.

title: STAT+: Which U.S. metros have the highest health spending? The answer might surprise you
summary: There's not a consistent theme among the 10 highest and lowest spending metros, which highlights the “irrationality” of U.S. health care spending, experts say.

title: Team approach to lowering high blood pressure worked even in ‘a tough landscape’
summary: A study is called “really, really important” in showing how teams can induce patients to get their blood pressure under control.

summary: Democrats are honing in on health care affordability as a key issue ahead of the midterm elections.

summary: The Trump administration will not be asking the Supreme Court to take up its fight to slash NIH support for research indirect costs

title: Smart contact lens monitors eye pressure and delivers glaucoma drugs in early tests
summary: A smart contact lens described in a research paper on Wednesday combines therapeutic and diagnostic capabilities for glaucoma patients.

summary: Ubl led PhRMA through the pandemic and a slew of efforts to rein in drug prices from Democrats and Republicans.

summary: Backed by OrbiMed, Novartis’ venture arm and many other investors, the biotech is among those working on newer technology that could broaden the reach of antibody-drug conjugates.

title: With 3 quick buyouts, Gilead leans into its latest transformation
summary: On a conference call with analysts, Gilead walked through how a string of recent deals focused on cancer and autoimmune conditions could help a diversification plan it’s long struggled to execute.

title: Activist investor revives campaign to overhaul Novavax board
summary: Shah Capital, which has agitated for change at the vaccine maker for years, is calling on fellow shareholders to end what it calls “dogmatic leadership” that’s led to the “destruction” of Novavax’s…

title: Jeito Capital, prominent biotech investor, raises $1.2B for next fund
summary: Jeito claimed the new bankroll, which will support 15 to 20 clinical-stage companies, is the largest ever for a “fully independent European fund dedicated to biopharma.”

title: STAT+: 23andMe finds genetic changes appear to help predict response to GLP-1 drugs for weight loss
summary: Changes in two genes appear to help predict whether patients will lose substantial weight on GLP-1 drugs — and whether the drugs will cause nausea or vomiting.

title: Novo underprices Lilly with higher-dose Wegovy; Insmed absorbs ‘expected’ study failure

title: Terns rebuffed a higher bid before selling to Merck
summary: Regulatory filings show that Terns ultimately accepted an offer 15% lower than a previous proposal as four companies vied for rights to the coveted leukemia drug developer.

title: Gilead continues M&A surge with $3.1B deal for ADC specialist Tubulis
summary: Gilead’s third acquisition since late February hands it a technology designed to make antibody-drug conjugates less toxic and multiple cancer medicines already in clinical testing.

title: Lipid nanoparticles deliver to the pancreas
